channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Cowboys beat Tampa Bay 26-24 as Baker Mayfield and the Bucs lose control of their playoff fate
item.description: Cooper Rush threw a touchdown pass, Brandon Aubrey kicked two 58-yarders among four field goals and the Dallas Cowboys beat Tampa Bay 26-24, costing Baker Mayfield and the Buccaneers control of their playoff fate.
item.dc:creator: Schuyler Dixon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bucs' Cody Mauch adjusting to and enjoying Tampa Bay's warm winters
item.description: Bucs' offensive lineman Cody Mauch is in his second year in the NFL, which means he is spending another Christmas in paradise.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 49ers eliminated from NFC playoffs before game against Miami. Cowboys, Saints, Cardinals also out
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — The defending NFC champion San Francisco 49ers were eliminated from the playoffs before their game at Miami began on Sunday because of victories by Washington and the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tyreek Hill says drops against 49ers were a result of lack of practice reps with Tua Tagovailoa
item.description: Tyreek Hill has not shied away from accountability during one of the least productive seasons of his nine-year career.
item.dc:creator: Alanis Thames, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Facing hometown Canucks 'a bit surreal' for Sharks rookie Macklin Celebrini
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Macklin Celebrini grew up dreaming about playing NHL hockey at Rogers Arena. On Monday night, that dream will become a reality. “Skating here when I was younger, I kind of tried to put myself in an NHL player’s situation or shoes, and pretend to be them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: John Wall gives broadcasting a try, while still holding out hope for one more NBA shot
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — John Wall was at the G League Winter Showcase last year, trying out, hoping to get back in the game. He did the same thing this year, without stepping foot on the court. The No.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caitlin Clark effect hasn't reversed the decades-long decline in girls basketball participation
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Plunging participation in girls basketball led one of Nebraska's largest high schools to cancel its varsity team for the year.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know for the Mizzou-Illinois Braggin' Rights game and how to watch
item.description: It's almost time for another Braggin' Rights matchup between two cross-state college basketball rivals: The Missouri Tigers and Illinois Fighting Illini.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Ryans, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz win back-to-back games for first time this season
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since April, the Utah Jazz are on a winning streak. Lauri Markkanen scored 21 points, Collin Sexton and Svi Mykhailiuk each had 18 points, and the Utah Jazz beat the Brooklyn Nets 105-94 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Northwestern demolishes DePaul in crosstown showdown
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. (WGN) — An early blitz on the offensive end and playmaking down the stretch from Brooks Barnhizer and Nick Martinelli helped seal a convincing win for Northwestern over DePaul in the yearly crosstown showdown between the two programs.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Yankees sign one-time MVP Paul Goldschmidt as they continue to pivot after losing Juan Soto: reports
item.description: The New York Yankees have reportedly signed first baseman Paul Goldschmidt to a one-year contract. Goldschmidt won the National League MVP award in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caprock Hockey hangs tough with defending State Champion Durango
item.description: In their second-ever varsity home game, the Caprock Eagles once again had a tough test, this time with the defending 4A State Champion Durango Demons.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Kucherov has 2 assists as Lightning beat Blues 3-1
item.description: Nikita Kucherov had a pair of assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to a 3-1 victory against the St. Louis Blues. Anthony Cirelli, Gage Goncalves and Nick Perbix scored for the Lightning, who have won six of their last seven. Andrei Vasilevskiy finished with 31 saves in the win.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ducks to honor fellow O.C. team at promotional night
item.description: If you're a fan of Orange County sports, you're in luck this January. On Jan. 7, the Anaheim Ducks are hosting Angels Night, an event that includes a baseball jersey giveaway for the first 5,000 fans in attendance. The game against the Calgary Flames begins at 7 p.m. Tickets begin at less than $30.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Notre Dame and Indiana get 1st taste of playoff football under the Friday night lights
item.description: Notre Dame hasn't made a national championship run since 1988. As for Indiana, the team didn't just win games this season. It dominated opponents.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 49ers suspend De'Vondre Campbell for 3 games
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco 49ers have suspended linebacker De'Vondre Campbell after he refused to play in Thursday’s game against the Los Angeles Rams, General Manager John Lynch announced Monday. The suspension is for the team's final three games of the season.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4
